  "concepts": [
    { "id": "c_ai_adoption", "name": "AI adoption in healthcare", "aliases": ["adoption of AI in healthcare"], "description": "The uptake of AI systems in real-world healthcare, characterized in the document as slow and limited.", "grounding_span": "the adoption of AI in real-world healthcare is still limited", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_trust", "name": "Trustworthiness of AI models", "aliases": ["trust", "trustworthy AI"], "description": "The reliability and credibility of AI models, cited as a main reason adoption is limited.", "grounding_span": "One of the main reasons is the trustworthiness of AI models", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_xai", "name": "Explainable AI (XAI)", "aliases": ["XAI", "Explainable Artificial Intelligence"], "description": "Techniques aiming to increase transparency of AI decision-making so users can understand and validate results.", "grounding_span": "Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) techniques aim to address these issues.", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_storytelling_xai", "name": "Storytelling XAI", "aliases": ["storytelling XAI framework"], "description": "An approach delivering end-to-end, audience-centric explanations by combining distillation, multi-task prediction, and interpretability.", "grounding_span": "we develop storytelling XAI", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_multitask_distillation", "name": "Multi-task distillation", "aliases": [], "description": "Combining multi-task prediction with knowledge distillation to exploit inter-task relationships.", "grounding_span": "combines multi-task distillation with interpretability techniques to enable audience-centric explainability", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_audience_centric", "name": "Audience-centric explainability", "aliases": ["audience-specific explanations"], "description": "Explanations tailored to the goals, expertise, and needs of a particular audience.", "grounding_span": "audience-centric explainability", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_concept_based", "name": "Concept-based explainability", "aliases": [], "description": "Methods that explain deep network behavior using human-understandable concepts such as texts.", "grounding_span": "Concept-based explainability methods aim to explain the behavior and predictions", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_kd", "name": "Knowledge distillation", "aliases": ["model distillation", "distillation"], "description": "Transferring knowledge from a large teacher model to a smaller student model without loss of performance.", "grounding_span": "Knowledge distillation refers to the process of transferring knowledge from a comparatively large model", "location": "Section 2.1" },
    { "id": "c_teacher", "name": "Teacher model", "aliases": ["teacher"], "description": "The complex large model whose knowledge is transferred to the student.", "grounding_span": "The complex large model is called teacher", "location": "Section 2.1" },
    { "id": "c_student", "name": "Student model", "aliases": ["student"], "description": "The smaller model that learns internal representations from the teacher.", "grounding_span": "the smaller model is calledstudent", "location": "Section 2.1" },
    { "id": "c_distillation_loss", "name": "Distillation loss", "aliases": [], "description": "Loss function that instructs the student to imitate the teacher's soft-target behavior.", "grounding_span": "to instruct the student model to imitate the teacher model’s behavior is called the distillation loss", "location": "Section 2.1" },
    { "id": "c_temperature", "name": "Temperature scaling in softmax", "aliases": ["temperature"], "description": "A parameter T softening the softmax probability distribution to expose inter-class correlations.", "grounding_span": "is the parameter of temperature", "location": "Section 2.1" },
    { "id": "c_mtl", "name": "Multi-task learning", "aliases": ["multi-task learning"], "description": "Teaching one model to handle several related tasks at once via shared representations.", "grounding_span": "multi-task learning is the process of teaching one model to handle several related tasks at once", "location": "Section 2.2" },
    { "id": "c_interpretability", "name": "Interpretability", "aliases": [], "description": "Understanding the connections between input features and model outputs and their relative importance.", "grounding_span": "Interpretability allows ML practitioners to understand the connections between the features", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_model_agnostic", "name": "Model-agnostic methods", "aliases": [], "description": "Interpretation methods flexible across model types, using approximation.", "grounding_span": "The two most common types of interpretation are model-agnostic methods and model-specific methods.", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_model_specific", "name": "Model-specific methods", "aliases": [], "description": "Interpretation methods designed for specific model types, giving accurate but less flexible interpretation.", "grounding_span": "Model-specific methods are designed to interpret specific types of models.", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_lime", "name": "LIME", "aliases": ["Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations"], "description": "A model-agnostic method approximating model behavior locally with interpretable surrogate models.", "grounding_span": "Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME)", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_shap", "name": "SHAP", "aliases": ["SHapley Additive Explanations", "SHapley Additive exPlanations"], "description": "A model-agnostic feature-attribution method based on Shapley values.", "grounding_span": "SHapley Additive Explanations (SHAP)", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_gradcam", "name": "GradCAM", "aliases": ["Grad-CAM", "GradCAM++"], "description": "A visualization (model-specific) technique producing class-discriminative saliency maps.", "grounding_span": "the most relevant being GradCAM", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_attention", "name": "Attention mechanism", "aliases": ["attention map visualization"], "description": "Mechanism used to visualize which parts of an input influence predictions, especially in text models.", "grounding_span": "Attention mechanisms are usually implemented to visualize text-based models.", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_cnn", "name": "Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)", "aliases": ["CNN"], "description": "Neural architecture interpreted via visualization techniques.", "grounding_span": "Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_rnn", "name": "Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)", "aliases": ["RNN"], "description": "Sequence architecture interpreted via attention mechanisms.", "grounding_span": "Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)", "location": "Section 2.3" },
    { "id": "c_counterfactual", "name": "Counterfactual explanations", "aliases": [], "description": "Explanations premised on feature independence, whose real-world plausibility is hard to measure.", "grounding_span": "Counterfactual explanations frequently rest on the presumptions that the features are independent", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_shapstories", "name": "SHAPstories and CFstories", "aliases": [], "description": "Techniques using LLMs to generate narratives explaining predictions from SHAP values and counterfactuals.", "grounding_span": "SHAPstories and CFstories, are two new techniques that use large language models to generate", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_narrative_xai", "name": "Narrative XAI via LLMs", "aliases": ["narrative XAI"], "description": "Use of large language models to produce narrative explanations of AI predictions.", "grounding_span": "application of the Large-Language-Models (LLMs) to enable narrative XAI", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_xaines", "name": "XAINES project", "aliases": ["XAINES"], "description": "A project exploring narratives to explain AI systems for diverse audiences.", "grounding_span": "An essential component of the XAINES approach is the narratives used to describe", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_chestxray", "name": "Chest X-ray analysis", "aliases": [], "description": "The healthcare use case used to demonstrate the framework.", "grounding_span": "the use case of chest X-ray analysis to demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework", "location": "Section 1" },
    { "id": "c_abnormality", "name": "Abnormality detection", "aliases": ["Chest X-ray abnormality detection"], "description": "Task of localizing and classifying chest abnormalities.", "grounding_span": "Chest X-ray abnormality detection", "location": "Section 4.1" },
    { "id": "c_report_gen", "name": "Report generation", "aliases": ["Chest X-ray report generation"], "description": "Task generating a radiology report serving as textual justification.", "grounding_span": "Chest X-ray report generation", "location": "Section 4.1" },
    { "id": "c_lung_seg", "name": "Lung segmentation", "aliases": [], "description": "Task of segmenting lungs from chest X-ray images.", "grounding_span": "Lung Segmentation: For this task", "location": "Section 4.1" },
    { "id": "c_resnet", "name": "ResNet50 backbone", "aliases": ["ResNet 50"], "description": "CNN backbone used in the abnormality detection and report generation teacher models.", "grounding_span": "a CNN-based model with ResNet 50 backbone", "location": "Section 4.2" },
    { "id": "c_image_captioning", "name": "Image captioning", "aliases": [], "description": "Concept underlying report generation, taking an image as input to produce text.", "grounding_span": "utilizes the concept of image-captioning, where an image is provided as an input", "location": "Section 4.2" },
    { "id": "c_shallow", "name": "Shallow models", "aliases": [], "description": "Low-depth models with reduced overfitting and better generalization on limited data.", "grounding_span": "the rationale behind the use of shallow models", "location": "Section 7" },
    { "id": "c_lrp", "name": "Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP)", "aliases": ["LRP"], "description": "An interpretability technique the framework could be extended to incorporate.", "grounding_span": "Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP)", "location": "Section 5" },
    { "id": "c_responsible_ai", "name": "Responsible AI", "aliases": [], "description": "AI whose trustworthiness supports responsible deployment.", "grounding_span": "to enable a responsible AI", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_domain_experts", "name": "Domain experts", "aliases": ["healthcare professionals"], "description": "Non-ML specialists (e.g., clinicians) who are a target audience for explanations.", "grounding_span": "hesitation of domain experts", "location": "Abstract" },
    { "id": "c_ml_practitioners", "name": "ML practitioners", "aliases": ["ML engineers"], "description": "Machine learning specialists who interpret model results in technical terms.", "grounding_span": "ML practitioners can also interpret the results", "location": "Section 6" },
    { "id": "c_cdss", "name": "Clinical decision support systems", "aliases": [], "description": "Systems into which XAI is integrated to align with medical ethical principles.", "grounding_span": "Integrating XAI into clinical decision support systems is important to align with core ethical principles", "location": "Section 3" },
    { "id": "c_hitl", "name": "Human-in-the-loop", "aliases": [], "description": "Planned user interface involving domain experts interacting with XAI results.", "grounding_span": "we would develop a user interface with a human-in-the-loop", "location": "Section 9" }
  ],
